Image 38: Proactio Proactio

Maker

@shabnam_katoch thanks so much, I’d love to hear how you get on! Making those graphics with my daughter was one of the best parts of this experience, and she’s thrilled to hear you like them☺️

Upvote (2)

Report

Share

3yr ago

Image 39: Shabnam Katoch

Shabnam Katoch

@jack_jenkins Thanks for letting me know :)

Upvote

Report

Share

3yr ago

Image 40: Nathan James

Nathan James

I absolutely love this. Looks like an excellent user experience (love that it shows how much more time they gained), and as a dad I fully understand the product. I'm always hesitant to pay for stuff for my kids though, just because they stop using basically anything after about 20 minutes.

Upvote [...] it's hard for them to wrap their head around time. It's so abstract, and the idea of "5 minutes left" means very little to them. 😅 When they want to go play, they're not exactly motivated to brush their teeth. I thought if I could just help them understand that there's plenty of time after all the "important" stuff is done, we'd all have stress-free mornings. 🙏 That's where Proactio comes into play. It turns a morning routine (or any other routine) into an engaging experience. - It's a visual timer that helps kids understand time, so they can use it well. - It's dynamic too, so kids can see "Play time" grow as they get things done. ✨ It was such fun working on it with they kids—yes, they've been alpha testers and helped pick the colors and sounds—and they love the final product. I
